channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us

item.title: NYC sightseeing helicopter plunges into river, killing 6, including family of Spanish tourists
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — A New York City sightseeing helicopter broke apart in midair Thursday and crashed upside-down into the Hudson River, killing the pilot and a family of five Spanish tourists in the latest U.S. aviation disaster, officials said.
item.dc:creator: MICHAEL R. SISAK, JENNIFER PELTZ and TED SHAFFREY, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: New Jersey woman identified as one of 200 Dominican Republic nightclub collapse victims
item.description: Family and friends of a New Jersey woman identified her as one of the more than 200 people who died in a nightclub collapse in Santo Domingo, Dominican Republic, on Tuesday.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: A jury finds Soulja Boy liable for abuse and sexual assault of ex-assistant, awards over $4 million
item.description: SANTA MONICA, Calif. (AP) — A jury on Thursday found that rapper Soulja Boy was liable for sexually assaulting and physically and emotionally abusing a former assistant, awarding the woman more than $4 million in damages.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Utah labor leaders in final push to repeal controversial collective bargaining ban
item.description: SALT LAKE CITY (ABC4) -- Labor leaders are racing against the clock as they now have less than a week to get enough signatures to place a referendum on the ballot to overturn one of this year’s most controversial bills – H.B. 267.
item.dc:creator: Yeonseung Kim, ABC 4

item.title: Gambian torture victims testify in US against a member of former dictator's military
item.description: DENVER (AP) — Suspected of backing a coup plot against the longtime dictator of Gambia nearly 20 years ago, Pharing Sanyang described Thursday how he was beaten with pipes and palm tree branches, pistol-whipped and hit in the face with a hammer.
item.dc:creator: COLLEEN SLEVIN, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Supreme Court says Trump administration must facilitate return of deported Maryland man
item.description: The Supreme Court on Thursday said the Trump administration must facilitate the return of a Maryland man who was mistakenly deported to El Salvador, rejecting the administration’s emergency appeal.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Movie theaters erupt in chaos as part of 'Minecraft' social media trend
item.description: Rowdy crowds of young people have been disrupting screenings of the newly released "A Minecraft Movie.
item.dc:creator: Rachel Tucker, Fox 5

item.title: Judge will halt Trump administration from ending humanitarian parole for people from four countries
item.description: BOSTON (AP) — A federal judge on Thursday will halt the Trump administration from ending a program that allowed hundreds of thousands of Cubans, Haitians, Nicaraguans and Venezuelans to temporarily live in the United States. U.S.
item.dc:creator: MICHAEL CASEY, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Meet the Gulf shrimpers rooting on Trump's tariffs in a Texas fishing town
item.description: While American consumers and markets wonder and worry about President Donald Trump’s on-again, off-again tariffs, Gulf coast shrimpers are cheering him on.
item.dc:creator: Lekan Oyekanmi And Jim Vertuno, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Annual White House Christmas ornament honors state dinner culinary diplomacy
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — The art of culinary diplomacy, practiced by presidents at White House state dinners for more than 150 years, is the theme of this year's official White House Christmas ornament.
item.dc:creator: DARLENE SUPERVILLE, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Trump's tariffs threaten to end quarter-century era of cheap goods for U.S. consumers
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — President Donald Trump’s new tariffs threaten to push up prices on clothes, mobile phones, furniture and many other products in the coming months, possibly ending the era of cheap goods that Americans enjoyed for about a quarter-century before the pandemic.
item.dc:creator: CHRISTOPHER RUGABER and ANNE D'INNOCENZIO, Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: NYC sightseeing helicopter plunges into river, killing 6, including family of Spanish tourists
item.description: A New York City sightseeing helicopter broke apart in midair and crashed into the Hudson River, killing six people, including a family of Spanish tourists, in the latest aviation disaster in the U.S.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Trump must face Central Park 5 defamation suit, judge rules
item.description: A federal judge declined to toss a defamation lawsuit against President Trump over his remarks last year about five men wrongly convicted of a 1989 rape, dubbed the Central Park Five.
item.dc:creator: Ella Lee, Fox 5

item.title: House Democrats warn Musk must leave government role by May 30
item.description: “In his short time in government, Elon Musk has done enormous harm to working Americans,” House Democrats said.
item.dc:creator: Julia Shapero, NewsNation

item.title: Trump orders agencies to ‘sunset’ environmental protections
item.description: The order applies to all regulations issued under laws governing things like energy appliance standards, mining and offshore drilling — as well as regulations issued under the Endangered Species Act.
item.dc:creator: Rachel Frazin, KRON4

item.title: Lawmakers say airplanes bumped each other on runway at Reagan National
item.description: Multiple lawmakers on Thursday said their airplane was bumped by another plane while waiting on the runway at Reagan National Airport.
item.dc:creator: Sarah Fortinsky, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Accused Austin Metcalf killer won't face death penalty or life without parole: DA
item.description: The teen accused of stabbing track star Austin Metcalf to death will not face life behind bars or the death penalty, according to a Texas district attorney.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Sweepstakes giant Publishers Clearing House files for bankruptcy
item.description: Publishers Clearing House was founded in 1953 in the basement of founder Harold Mertz in Long Island, New York.
item.dc:creator: Joshua Hallenbeck, NewsNation

item.title: Giant bust of Elon Musk's head vandalized near Starbase in Texas
item.description: "We don't want this to happen again because it was an effort to put it up and very expensive," the owner said.
item.dc:creator: Mia Morales, NewsNation

item.title: Budget airline Avelo to fly deportation flights for ICE from Arizona
item.description: Budget carrier Avelo Airlines signed an agreement to fly federal deportation flights from Arizona starting in May, according to the company, whose founder acknowledged the decision may be controversial.
item.dc:creator: Susan Haigh, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Egg prices jump to record high despite Trump's predictions and bird flu slowing
item.description: The increase reported Thursday in the Consumer Price Index means consumers and businesses that rely on eggs should not anticipate immediate relief.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Jury awards $1.68 billion to women who accused writer and director James Toback of sexual abuse
item.description: The judgement marks one of the largest jury awards since the advent of the #MeToo movement, as well as in New York state history
item.dc:creator: Susan Haigh, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Sweepstakes giant Publishers Clearing House files for bankruptcy
item.description: Publishers Clearing House, famous for its big checks and sweepstakes, files for Chapter 11 bankruptcy as it shifts to digital advertising.
item.dc:creator: Joshua Hallenbeck, Fox 5

item.title: Jewish families channel Passover story after devastating losses in Los Angeles wildfires
item.description: PASADENA, California (AP) — Aty Rotter lost her family home and her spiritual home in the ravenous fire that scorched a large swath of Los Angeles County earlier this year.
item.dc:creator: DEEPA BHARATH, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Author Jillian Lauren, wife of Weezer bassist, arrested after being shot by police
item.description: Jillian Lauren, author and wife of Weezer bassist Scott Shriner, was shot and injured by Los Angeles police and arrested on suspicion of attempted murder after pointing a gun at officers from her front yard, authorities said.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Russian-American ballerina from Los Angeles released from Russia
item.description: A U.S.-Russian dual national imprisoned in Russia on treason charges was freed Thursday in exchange for a Russian man jailed on smuggling charges in the U.S., a prisoner swap that comes as Moscow and Washington have made efforts to repair ties.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, KTLA 5

item.title: Hope of finding survivors fades in aftermath of Dominican club roof collapse
item.description: SANTO DOMINGO, Dominican Republic (AP) — Rescue crews in the Dominican Republic on Thursday dug through the remains of a legendary nightclub whose roof collapsed earlier this week, killing at least 184 people, but hope of finding survivors was slim.
item.dc:creator: MARTÍN ADAMES ALCÁNTARA and DÁNICA COTO Associated Press, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Texas lottery player claims he was cheated out of $95 million jackpot win
item.description: A Texas lottery player filed a lawsuit in Travis County on Tuesday alleging his May 2023 Lotto Texas jackpot prize was severely diminished by $95 million because a group of lottery retailers and a London-based sports gambling company conspired to rig a lottery drawing less than a month before his wi
item.dc:creator: Dylan McKim, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: VP of Bikers Against Child Abuse chapter arrested for sex crimes against children
item.description: He was charged with promoting a sexual performance by a child and possessing a sexual performance by a child.
item.dc:creator: Kayleigh Hunter-Gasperini, NewsNation

item.title: State leaders tell Trump cabinet members that Utah should run its own federal programs
item.description: Utah's Speaker of the House Mike Schultz is among the top state leaders pushing for Utah to have control over federal programs.
item.dc:creator: Lindsay Aerts, ABC 4

item.title: Pennsylvania judge is convicted of shooting and wounding her ex-boyfriend as he slept
item.description: HARRISBURG, Pa. (AP) — A magistrate judge in Pennsylvania accused of shooting and wounding her ex-boyfriend in the head as he slept was convicted Wednesday of attempted homicide and aggravated assault charges.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: US Postal Service seeks to hike cost of a first-class stamp
item.description: If approved, the increase for a “forever” stamp and similar increases for postcards, metered letters and international mail would take effect July 13.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: LeBron James becomes first pro male athlete to have likeness depicted in Ken doll
item.description: LeBron James has become the first professional male athlete to have his likeness depicted in a Ken doll.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Trump tariffs worry US shoppers who buy groceries at Asian supermarkets
item.description: LOS ANGELES (AP) — Loyal customers of Asian supermarkets and other grocery stores that specialize in selling imported food heaved a collective sigh of dismay when President Donald Trump announced extra-high U.S. tariffs on goods from dozens of countries.
item.dc:creator: TERRY TANG and KARENA PHAN, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Trump administration appeals court decision ordering AP journalists back into White House events
item.description: The Trump administration said Wednesday it would appeal a federal court decision that ordered it to re-admit Associated Press journalists to White House events on First Amendment grounds.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Trump limits tariffs on most nations for 90 days, except China
item.description: Facing a global market meltdown, President Donald Trump on Wednesday abruptly backed down on his tariffs on most nations for 90 days, but raised his tax rate on Chinese imports to 125%.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, KTLA 5

item.title: Universal Studios announces plans to build first European theme park resort
item.description: Universal Destinations & Experiences officially announced on Tuesday that it intends to build its first-ever theme park and resort in the United Kingdom. In 2023, Universal purchased a 476-acre parcel of land outside Bedford, England, about 55 miles north of London.
item.dc:creator: Iman Palm, KTLA 5
